title: Columnea selloana
intro: Columnea selloana is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, commonly known as the 'False Glory Flower' or 'Sello's Columnea', native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Columnea selloana include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and other health conditions.
taxonomy: Columnea selloana is classified within the family Gesneriaceae. This family includes a wide range of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. Columnea selloana is further grouped into the genus Columnea, which comprises over 100 species of flowering plants. These plants are known for their vibrant flowers and are often grown as ornamental plants. They are native to Central and South America, specifically in the tropical regions.
